- Goal model: Navigate through the maze from the player's starting position to the target position using UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT.

- Plan: Try RIGHT again to continue along the corridor, then map the full path.

sections.content: Let me check the player's position after the two RIGHT moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position after 2 RIGHT moves
after_seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in after_seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] == 'b' and node['pixels'] == 24:
        print(f"Player: boundary={node['boundary']}")

# Check the area around the player
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Around player (rows 7-15, cols 30-50) ===")
for i in range(7, 16):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][30:50]}")

# Also check below to see if there's a path DOWN
print("\n=== Below player (rows 13-25, cols 30-50) ===")
for i in range(13, 26):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][30:50]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Now I can see the player is at rows 8-12, cols 38-42. Looking at the maze:
- Row 12: `BBBBBBBBbbbbbBWWWWWW` (player at cols 38-42)
- Row 13: `BBBBBBBBBBRBBBWWWWWW` (red area at cols 39-40, black at cols 38, 41-42)
- Row 14: `BBWWWWWWWBRBWWWWWWWW` (white path starts here)
The player is near the right edge of the corridor. The red area (R) is at cols 39-40, which seems to be a vertical wall on the right side of the maze.
Looking at the maze structure:
- The player is in a horizontal corridor at the top (rows 8-12)
- To the right of the player is white (W) area, which seems to be outside the maze
- Below the player, there's a red area and then white paths
I think the player needs to go DOWN now to enter the main maze area. But earlier, DOWN was blocked. Let me check if the player can now move DOWN from this position.
Actually, looking at the maze more carefully:
- Row 13: `BBBBBBBBBBRBBBWWWWWW` - there's black (B) at cols 30-38 and 41-42, with red (R) at cols 39-40
- The player is at cols 38-42, so the player overlaps with the red area
Wait, the player is at cols 38-42, and row 13 has:
- Cols 30-38: B (black)
- Cols 39-40: R (red)
- Cols 41-42: B (black)
- Cols 43-49: W (white)
So the player's bottom edge is at row 12, and row 13 has black at cols 38 and 41-42, with red at cols 39-40. The player might be able to move DOWN if there's a path.
Let me try moving DOWN from this position.
